basic_details.job_title: NBRC Research Associate-I Recruitment 2026 - Apply Offline
basic_details.short_information: National Brain Research Centre (NBRC) Recruitment 2026: National Brain Research Centre has released the official notification for 1 Research Associate-I posts, inviting applications from eligible candidates. This recruitment provides an excellent opportunity for candidates seeking stable and well-paid research jobs.

age_limit.details: Maximum Age Limit: 40 years Age relaxation will be as per government rules.

qualification.education_qualification: Ph.D/MD or equivalent degree with at least one research article published in a reputed and indexed journal.
qualification.details: Research Associate - I: Ph.D/MD or equivalent degree with at least one research article published in a reputed and indexed journal. Experience: Animal handling, preferably birds. Histology, cryosectioning and microscopy. Stereotaxic surgeries on small animals. Performing and analysing behavioural experiments.

salary_details.details: Research Associate - I: Rs. 58,000/- pm + 20% HRA (as applicable). No other allowances/benefits will be admissible.

how_to_apply: Candidates who wish to apply for NBRC Research Associate-I Recruitment 2026 through offline mode should follow the steps below.
Read the official notification carefully before applying.
Download the application form from the official website: .
Fill the application form carefully with correct information.
Attach self-attested copies of required documents such as photograph, signature, ID proof, and certificates.
Check all details carefully before submission.
Send the completed application form to the address mentioned in the notification through Speed Post / Registered Post or as instructed.
Ensure the application reaches before the last date: 10-06-2026.
Keep a photocopy of the submitted application form for future reference.

faq.question: Q1. What is the last date to apply for the NBRC Research Associate-I recruitment?
faq.answer: The last date to submit the application for the NBRC Research Associate-I position is 10th June 2026. Ensure your application reaches the specified address by this date.

faq.question: Q2. What is the educational qualification required for the Research Associate-I post?
faq.answer: Candidates must possess a Ph.D/MD or an equivalent degree. Additionally, they should have at least one research article published in a reputed and indexed journal to be eligible.

faq.question: Q3. What is the age limit for the Research Associate-I position?
faq.answer: The maximum age limit for the Research Associate-I post is 40 years. Age relaxation will be provided as per government norms.

faq.question: Q4. What is the salary offered for the Research Associate-I role?
faq.answer: The selected candidate for the Research Associate-I position will receive a salary of Rs. 58,000/- per month plus 20% House Rent Allowance (HRA).

faq.question: Q5. How can I apply for the NBRC Research Associate-I recruitment?
faq.answer: Applications need to be submitted offline. You must download the application form, fill it correctly, and send it along with self-attested copies of certificates to the Administrative Officer (Academics), NBRC.

faq.question: Q6. What is the application fee for this recruitment?
faq.answer: The application fee is Rs. 200/-. This can be paid via Demand Draft in favour of Director, BRIC-NBRC payable at Manesar, or through the payment link available on the website via QR code.

faq.question: Q7. What is the selection process for the Research Associate-I post?
faq.answer: The selection process involves two stages: an initial application screening followed by an interview for shortlisted candidates.

faq.question: Q8. Is this a permanent government job?
faq.answer: This is a project-based recruitment. The position is for a Research Associate-I under a DBT-funded project.

faq.question: Q9. Where is the National Brain Research Centre located?
faq.answer: The National Brain Research Centre (NBRC) is located at NH-8, Nainwal Mode, Manesar, Haryana 122052.

faq.question: Q10. What specific experience is preferred for this role?
faq.answer: Preferred experience includes animal handling (especially birds), histology, cryosectioning, microscopy, performing stereotaxic surgeries on small animals, and conducting/analysing behavioural experiments.
